mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses biological mechanisms of senescence including cellular ageing, telomere shortening, evolutionary theories of why organisms age, and life span -- covering all major sub-topics of aging and decline in animals.

Mitochondrial dysfunction is implicated in ageing and disease, and mitochondrial involvement in programmed cell death (apoptosis) is directly relevant to causes of aging and control/integration of development.
Circadian rhythms involve clock genes and molecular feedback loops controlling biological timing of developmental processes, directly relevant to the control and integration of development.

The infant brain episode covers postembyonic development including neuroplasticity, synaptogenesis and synaptic pruning, critical periods for development, and the nature-nurture interplay -- directly relevant to postembryonic development and differentiation.
